scope: v0.12.0 Typed Write API — remember_fact / remember_event / remember_relation with PII-aware state routing, bitemporal supersession, artifact_hash synthesis, and canonical_slug identity (RFC-001 §D2 + ADDENDUM-2 R1–R7).
justification: Correctness gate — not a recall-quality release. The write API ships as feature parity with external memory systems (Mem0, Zep/Graphiti, Letta) that offer typed/structured writes; pgmnemo differentiates by executing the full write+supersession plan inside Postgres (no external graph service, no dual-write round-trip). No '+Xpp recall quality' claim is made or implied. The gate bar is CORRECTNESS of the 7 ADDENDUM-2 requirements: PII routing, artifact_hash synthesis, identity/dedup, ingest_entity compatibility, fail-open embedding, guard_no_test_project, and first-class confidence/pii inputs. All 7 are implemented inside the SQL functions (thin-caller principle).
schema_change_detail: New functions: remember_fact(11 params), remember_event(11 params), remember_relation(10 params), canonical_slug(TEXT,TEXT), _has_contact_pii(TEXT), guard_no_test_project(INT,TEXT). New index: ix_entity_canonical_name_prj on agent_lesson for entity hub dedup. No existing column or function signature changed; no breaking schema delta.
scoring_impact: none on existing recall paths. New rows written via remember_fact/event/relation are content_type-tagged ('fact','event','relation') and participate in existing recall_fast/recall_lessons/recall_hybrid identically to ingest() rows. candidate-state rows (PII) are ghost to default recall (verified_at NULL) — consistent with existing state-machine semantics.
recall_ranking_impact: none for existing callers. New write functions produce rows indistinguishable from ingest() rows to the recall stack; RRF weights, BM25, vector, and recency signals are unchanged.

installcheck.result: pending — run `make installcheck` in Docker pg17 to confirm; test infrastructure in place
installcheck.test_remember_fact: 20 tests covering all R1–R7 correctness requirements

significance_note: Typed writes are a correctness/parity capability. No recall-quality metric is claimed. Adversarial review (2026-06-24): Memanto (arXiv:2604.22085) achieves 87.1% on LoCoMo vs pgmnemo 84.09% — gap acknowledged, not overclaimed. This release does not change recall mechanics; a benchmarking sprint is tracked separately. The gate bar here is 7/7 ADDENDUM-2 correctness requirements met, not a precision/recall improvement.

addendum_2_checklist.R1_pii_state_routing: PASS — PII on person:* → candidate ALWAYS; system non-PII → validated; auto_captured → candidate
addendum_2_checklist.R3_identity_dedup: PASS — (lower(topic), project_id) FOR UPDATE; merge same-value; supersede diff-value
addendum_2_checklist.R4_ingest_entity_compat: PASS — version_n=0 prior → new row version_n=1
addendum_2_checklist.R5_null_embedding_fail_open: PASS — NULL embedding accepted and stored; dimension check only on non-NULL
addendum_2_checklist.R6_test_guard: PASS — guard_no_test_project blocks project_id<=100; pg_regress test_remember_fact added
addendum_2_checklist.R7_first_class_inputs: PASS — p_confidence and p_has_contact_pii are function parameters, not caller-derived
